en.extract:
The red-billed oxpecker (Buphagus erythrorynchus) is a passerine bird in the oxpecker family, Buphagidae. It is native to the eastern savannah of sub-Saharan Africa, from the Central African Republic east to South Sudan and south to northern and eastern South Africa. It is more widespread than the yellow-billed oxpecker in Southern Africa, where their ranges overlap.


Taxonomy
The scientific name comes from Ancient Greek βοῦς (boûs), meaning "ox", and φάγος (phágos), meaning "eater". The specific name comes from ἐρυθρός (eruthrós), meaning "red", and ῥύγχος (rhúnkhos), meaning "snout".


Distribution
The red-billed oxpecker is a native of the savanna of sub-Saharan Africa. It ranges across Ethiopia and Somalia through Kenya, Tanzania, Malawi, and Zambia to southern Africa, Botswana, Zimbabwe, southern Mozambique, and north-eastern South Africa.


Description
A juvenile oxpecker is darker brown than its parents. Its bill is dark olive at first, but gradually takes on adult colouration after four months. Its flight is strong and direct, and their call is a hissy crackling trik-quisss.


Behaviour

The red-billed oxpecker nests in tree holes lined with hair plucked from livestock. It lays two to five eggs, with three being the average. Outside the breeding season it forms large, chattering flocks.
The preferred habitat is open country, and the red-billed oxpecker eats insects. Both the English and scientific names arise from this species' habit of perching on large wild and domesticated mammals such as cattle and eating ticks. This species's relationship with rhinoceros gives the Swahili name Askari wa kifaru meaning "the rhino's guard".
An adult will take nearly 100 blood-engorged female Rhipicephalus (Boophilus) decoloratus ticks, or more than 12,000 larvae in a day. However, their preferred food is blood, and while they may take ticks bloated with blood, they also feed on it directly, pecking at the mammal's wounds to keep them open.
Field observations in rhinoceros have shown oxpeckers warning the nearsighted rhinoceros of danger.


References


Further reading
Feare, Chris; Craig, Adrian (1999). Starlings and Mynas. Princeton University Press. ISBN 0-7136-3961-X.
Zuccon, Dario; Cibois, Anne; Pasquet, Eric; Ericson, Per G. P. (November 2006). "Nuclear and Mitochondrial Sequence Data Reveal the Major Lineages of Starlings, Mynas and Related Taxa". Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution. 41 (2): 333–344. Bibcode:2006MolPE..41..333Z. doi:10.1016/j.ympev.2006.05.007. PMID 16806992.


External links
Red-billed oxpecker species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds
Red-billed oxpecker videos, photos & sounds on the Macaulay Library

The '''red-billed oxpecker''' ('''''Buphagus erythrorynchus''''') is a [[passerine]] [[Aves|bird]] in the oxpecker family, [[Buphagidae]]. It is native to the eastern savannah of sub-[[Sahara]]n [[Africa]], from the [[Central African Republic]] east to [[South Sudan]] and south to northern and eastern [[South Africa]]. It is more widespread than the [[yellow-billed oxpecker]] in Southern Africa, where their ranges overlap.

== Taxonomy ==
The scientific name comes from [[Ancient Greek]] {{lang|grc|βοῦς}} ({{lang|grc-Latn|boûs}}), meaning "ox", and {{lang|grc|φάγος}} ({{lang|grc-Latn|phágos}}), meaning "eater". The [[specific name]] comes from {{lang|grc|ἐρυθρός}} ({{lang|grc-Latn|eruthrós}}), meaning "red", and {{lang|grc|ῥύγχος}} ({{lang|grc-Latn|rhúnkhos}}), meaning "snout".

== Distribution ==
The red-billed oxpecker is a native of the savanna of sub-Saharan Africa. It ranges across [[Ethiopia]] and [[Somalia]] through [[Kenya]], [[Tanzania]], [[Malawi]], and [[Zambia]] to southern Africa, [[Botswana]], [[Zimbabwe]], southern [[Mozambique]], and north-eastern [[South Africa]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.biodiversityexplorer.org/birds/sturnidae/buphagus_erythrorhynchus.htm|title=Buphagus erythrorhynchus (Red-billed oxpecker)|website=www.biodiversityexplorer.org}}</ref>

== Description ==
A juvenile oxpecker is darker brown than its parents. Its bill is dark olive at first, but gradually takes on adult colouration after four months.{{Cn|date=June 2023}} Its flight is strong and direct, and their call is a hissy crackling ''trik-quisss''.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.beautyofbirds.com/redbilledoxpeckers.html|title=Red-billed Oxpeckers – Beauty of Birds|website=www.beautyofbirds.com|date=16 September 2021 }}</ref>

== Behaviour ==
[[File:Red-billed Oxpecker Eggs JM.jpg|thumb|Clutch in a nest lined with [[impala]] hair, [[Kenya]]]]

The red-billed oxpecker nests in tree holes lined with hair plucked from [[livestock]]. It lays two to five [[Bird egg|eggs]], with three being the average. Outside the breeding season it forms large, chattering [[flock (birds)|flock]]s.

The preferred habitat is open country, and the red-billed oxpecker eats [[insect]]s. Both the English and scientific names arise from this species' habit of perching on large wild and domesticated [[mammal]]s such as [[cattle]] and eating [[tick]]s.<ref>{{cite journal|author1=Mikula P |author2=Hadrava J |author3=Albrecht T |author4=Tryjanowski P. |date=March 19, 2018 |title=Large-Scale Assessment of Commensalistic–Mutualistic Associations Between African Birds and Herbivorous Mammals Using Internet Photos |journal= PeerJ|volume=6 |article-number=e4520 |doi=10.7717/peerj.4520 |doi-access=free |pmc=5863707 |pmid=29576981}}</ref> This species's relationship with [[rhinoceros]] gives the [[Swahili language|Swahili]] name {{Lang|sw|Askari wa kifaru}} meaning "the rhino's guard".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shoot |first=Britta |date=10 June 2020 |title=These Birds Protect Black Rhinos from Poachers (but Also Drink Their Blood) |url=https://www.audubon.org/news/these-birds-protect-black-rhinos-poachers-also-drink-their-blood |publisher=[[Audubon]] |access-date=6 April 2022}}</ref>

An adult will take nearly 100 blood-engorged female ''[[Rhipicephalus|Rhipicephalus (Boophilus) decoloratus]]'' ticks, or more than 12,000 [[larva]]e in a day. However, their preferred food is blood, and while they may take ticks bloated with blood, they also feed on it directly, pecking at the mammal's wounds to keep them open.

Field observations in [[rhinoceros]] have shown oxpeckers warning the nearsighted rhinoceros of danger.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Plotz |first1=Roan D. |last2=Linklater |first2=Wayne L. |date=18 May 2020 |url=https://www.cell.com/current-biology/pdf/S0960-9822(20)30353-5.pdf |title=Oxpeckers Help Rhinos Evade Humans |journal=Current Biology |volume=30 |issue=10 |pages=1965–1969.e2 |bibcode=2020CBio...30E1965P |doi=10.1016/j.cub.2020.03.015 |doi-access=free |pmid=32275876}}</ref>

File:Giraffe Oxpeckers Lupande Zambia Jul23 A7R 06194.jpg|Row of red-billed oxpeckers on a [[Thornicroft's giraffe]], [[Zambia]]

</gallery>

zh.extract:
紅嘴牛椋鳥（學名：Buphagus erythrorhynchus）屬於雀形目椋鳥科椋鳥屬，是一種分布於非洲的牛椋鳥。常棲息於斑馬，非洲水牛，犀牛，巨羚，黑斑瞪羚，河馬，長頸鹿，甚至大象身上，尋找毛髮皮膚中的寄生蟲，扁蝨和皮屑。足型為對稱型，即兩趾朝前，兩趾朝後，這樣就能以任何角度站在草食動物光滑的皮膚表面上。分布於非洲中南部（阿拉伯半島南部，和撒哈拉沙漠以南大部分地區）。紅嘴牛椋鳥是一種季節性繁殖的鳥類，築巢於3~5月和10~12月，在肯亞則於5~7月份繁殖。通常於植物的枝幹上棄置的啄木鳥巢中繁殖。牠們通常成對生活，不過據紀錄，此物種也常成群生活。
生活型態與食性：通常成對生活，不過也會成群生活。常常站在草食動物的身上，啄食寄生蟲，扁蝨與皮屑。
分布：主要分布於非洲中南部的草原以及灌木叢。

繁殖：紅嘴牛椋鳥是季節性繁殖的鳥類，築巢於3~5月和10~12月，在肯亞則於5~7月份繁殖。
